title: Kommo Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Kommo** (formerly amoCRM) is a CRM platform exposing a tenant-scoped REST API at `https://{subdomain}.kommo.com/api/v4`. This connector â€” `KommoConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "kommo"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) â€” wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Account | `/account` | Health probe; verify token + subdomain. |
| Leads | `/leads` | List, get, create, update, delete leads (the deal pipeline). |
| Contacts | `/contacts` | List, get, create, update, delete contacts. |
| Companies | `/companies` | List, get, create, update, delete companies. |
| Customers | `/customers` | List customers (repeat-buyer CRM). |
| Tasks | `/tasks` | List, get, create, update, delete tasks. |
| Events | `/events` | List CRM timeline events. |
| Notes | `/{entity_type}/{id}/notes` | List + create notes per entity. |
| Custom Fields | `/{entity_type}/custom_fields` | List + create custom fields. |
| Pipelines | `/leads/pipelines` | List pipelines + statuses. |
| Users | `/users` | List operator users. |
| Webhooks | `/webhooks` | Create + delete outbound webhooks. |

The connector normalises leads + contacts into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC â€” all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::KommoHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Kommo long-lived access token

1. Sign in to your Kommo account at `https://{subdomain}.kommo.com`.
2. Open **Settings â†’ Integrations** (top-right gear icon).
3. Click **Create Integration â†’ Private Integration** (or open an existing one).
4. Configure the integration â€” name, description, optional redirect URI.
5. Tick the **Access Rights** your team needs (read/write leads, contacts, etc.).
6. Open the **Keys and Scopes** tab on the saved integration.
7. Copy the **Long-Lived Access Token** â€” Kommo shows it on the integration page.
8. Note your **subdomain** â€” the `mycompany` portion of `https://mycompany.kommo.com`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP â†’ Manage Connectors â†’ Kommo**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `subdomain` (text) | `mycompany` (just the subdomain or the full URL â€” both are accepted) |
| `access_token` (secret) | The long-lived token from step 7 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override the computed `https://{subdomain}.kommo.com/api/v4` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 |
| `timeout_s` (number) | Per-request timeout, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `subdomain` + `access_token` are present (it does NOT call the API), sanitises the subdomain (strips protocol + `.kommo.com` suffix), persists via `save_config`, rebuilds the `KommoHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /account` as a lightweight health check. On 2xx â†’ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 â†’ `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 â†’ `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 â†’ `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Kommo REST API supports a long-lived OAuth access token for server-to-server integrations. Shielva treats it as an API key (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) â€” no OAuth code exchange, no refresh-token dance.

### Credential model

- `access_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <access_token>`.
- `subdomain` is captured at install time and used to construct the per-tenant base URL `https://{subdomain}.kommo.com/api/v4` â€” no header.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `subdomain` + `access_token`. Sanitises subdomain. Persists config. Does **not** call any Kommo endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` wrapping the configured long-lived token. No code exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /api/v4/account` â€” minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called â€” no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None â€” the access_token lives in `self.config` only. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

### Subdomain handling

`helpers.utils.sanitize_subdomain` accepts any of:

- `mycompany`
- `mycompany.kommo.com`
- `https://mycompany.kommo.com/`
- `HTTPS://MyCompany.kommo.com`

and returns the canonical bare token `mycompany`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` â€” never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `subdomain` | text | yes | `self.subdomain` (sanitised) | The `{subdomain}` segment in `{subdomain}.kommo.com` |
| `access_token` | secret | yes | `self.access_token` | `Bearer <token>` in Authorization header |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` | Override of the computed base URL |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.timeout_s` (default 30) | Per-request httpx timeout |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["subdomain", "access_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `KommoConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::KommoHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `subdomain` + `access_token`; sanitise subdomain; persist via `save_config`; rebuild `KommoHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth â€” returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured long-lived token (`token_type="api_key"`).

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /account`. Classifies failures: 401 â†’ `OFFLINE/TOKEN_EXPIRED`; 403 â†’ `UNHEALTHY/INVALID_CREDENTIALS`; network â†’ `OFFLINE/CONNECTED`; other â†’ `DEGRADED/CONNECTED`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Paginates Kommo leads (`updated_at` cursor) â†’ `NormalizedDocument` â†’ `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Leads
children.content:
### `async list_leads(page=1, limit=50, query=None, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /leads?page=&limit=&query=&filter[â€¦]`. Nested `filter` dicts are flattened to Kommo bracket-style query params (e.g. `{"statuses": [{"pipeline_id": 1}]}` â†’ `filter[statuses][0][pipeline_id]=1`).

### `async get_lead(lead_id) -> Dict`
`GET /leads/{id}`.

### `async create_lead(leads: List[Dict]) -> Dict`
`POST /leads` â€” body MUST be a JSON array (Kommo bulk semantics). Raises `TypeError` if a single dict is passed.

### `async update_lead(lead_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /leads/{id}` with a single object body.

### `async delete_lead(lead_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /leads/{id}`.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(page=1, limit=50, query=None) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts`.

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts/{id}`.

### `async create_contact(contacts: List[Dict]) -> Dict`
`POST /contacts` â€” array body.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /contacts/{id}`.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /contacts/{id}`.

children.title: Companies + Customers
children.content:
### `async list_companies(page=1, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /companies`.

### `async get_company(company_id) -> Dict`
`GET /companies/{id}`.

### `async create_company(companies: List[Dict]) -> Dict`
`POST /companies` â€” array body.

### `async update_company(company_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /companies/{id}`.

### `async delete_company(company_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /companies/{id}`.

### `async list_customers(page=1, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /customers`.

children.title: Tasks + Events + Notes
children.content:
### `async list_tasks(page=1, limit=50, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /tasks`.

### `async get_task(task_id) -> Dict`, `async create_task(tasks: List[Dict]) -> Dict`, `async update_task(task_id, fields) -> Dict`, `async delete_task(task_id) -> Dict`
Full CRUD on `/tasks` â€” `create_task` requires array body.

### `async list_events(page=1, limit=50, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /events` â€” read-only timeline.

### `async list_notes(entity_type, entity_id, page=1, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /{entity_type}/{entity_id}/notes` â€” works for `leads`, `contacts`, `companies`, `customers`.

### `async create_note(entity_type, entity_id, notes: List[Dict]) -> Dict`
`POST /{entity_type}/{entity_id}/notes` â€” array body.

children.title: Custom Fields Â· Pipelines Â· Users Â· Webhooks
children.content:
### `async list_custom_fields(entity_type) -> Dict`
`GET /{entity_type}/custom_fields`.

### `async create_custom_field(entity_type, fields: List[Dict]) -> Dict`
`POST /{entity_type}/custom_fields` â€” array body.

### `async list_pipelines() -> Dict`
`GET /leads/pipelines`.

### `async list_users() -> Dict`
`GET /users`.

### `async create_webhook(destination, settings=None) -> Dict`
`POST /webhooks` â€” body `{destination, settings: [event_names...]}`.

### `async delete_webhook(destination) -> Dict`
`DELETE /webhooks` â€” body identifies the webhook by destination URL.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
KommoError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
â”śâ”€â”€ KommoAuthError                      # 401 / 403
â”śâ”€â”€ KommoBadRequestError                # 400
â”śâ”€â”€ KommoNotFoundError                  # 404
â”śâ”€â”€ KommoConflictError                  # 409
â”śâ”€â”€ KommoRateLimitError                 # 429 â€” retry_after_s
â””â”€â”€ KommoServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
KommoNetworkError = KommoServerError    # legacy name from older code
KommoNotFound     = KommoNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` if present; otherwise exponential backoff `min(1.0 * 2 ** attempt + jitter, 32.0)`. Retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`. |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `KommoServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 â†’ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 â†’ ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED) â€” logged as warning
5xx / network â†’ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other â†’ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `subdomain` or `access_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. The token is shown only once at generation â€” rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Kommo â€” token lacks scope | In Kommo â†’ Settings â†’ Integrations â†’ your integration, edit and grant the read/write rights your operations need; re-save the integration. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 â€” token revoked or wrong subdomain | Verify the subdomain matches the account that owns the token. Rotate the token via the integration page. |
| `kommo.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting Kommo's ~7 req/sec rate cap | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer calls. |
| 404 on `/leads/{id}` | Wrong entity ID OR the lead lives in a sub-account | Verify the ID via `list_leads`; check the subdomain matches the account holding the lead. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'KommoNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed â€” `KommoNetworkError = KommoServerError` alias added. |
| `ValueError: subdomain is required` at startup | `subdomain` field blank | Fill the `subdomain` install field â€” the HTTP client cannot build a base URL without it. |
| `create_lead expects a listâ€¦` TypeError | Caller passed a single lead dict | Wrap in a list â€” Kommo's `/leads`, `/contacts`, `/companies`, `/tasks`, `/notes`, `/custom_fields` all expect array bodies for bulk semantics. |
